creators.creative_thesis: I make calm digital things that feel like looking out a window.
Answering specific questions with entire websites.
If it makes me laugh, I ship it.

creators.bio: Cat dad, amateur architect of silly things
creators.creative_thesis: I build things my cat would be proud of.
I build worlds because reality doesn't have enough lore.
Automating the things nobody should have to do manually.
I make sounds for places that don't exist yet.

creators.bio: Open source connoisseur and README sommelier
creators.creative_thesis: Your README deserves better. Or worse. Depends.
I build things that make people feel seen.
I turn my worst habits into apps.
Preserving things the internet forgot it made.
If it can be an API, it should be an API.

projects.name: Color of the Day
projects.one_line_pitch: Every day, one color. That's it. That's the site.
projects.about: A meditative site that generates one unique color per day based on the date, weather data, and vibes. No controls. No settings. Just today's color.
projects.why_i_made_this: I wanted to make something that felt like looking out a window. No clicks, no decisions. Just a color.

projects.name: Is it Raining in Tokyo?
projects.one_line_pitch: A single page that tells you if it's raining in Tokyo right now.
projects.about: Checks real weather API data for Tokyo and displays a big YES or NO. Has a subtle rain animation when it is, in fact, raining.
projects.why_i_made_this: My friend in Tokyo kept telling me about the rain. I wanted to just know, instantly, without opening a weather app.

projects.name: The Apology Generator
projects.one_line_pitch: AI-generated apologies for things you definitely shouldn't have done.
projects.about: Type in what you did wrong and get a dramatically over-the-top, professionally worded apology letter. Share it as a link.
projects.why_i_made_this: I apologize too much. I wanted AI to apologize better than me.

projects.name: Font Feelings
projects.one_line_pitch: Describe your mood and get a font pairing that matches.
projects.about: An emotional font recommender. Describe how you feel, and it suggests a Google Font pairing with a color palette and sample layout.
projects.why_i_made_this: Every font picker sorts by popularity. None sort by feeling. That seemed wrong.

projects.name: Pet Name Rater
projects.one_line_pitch: Submit your pet's name and an AI will rate it out of 10 with brutal honesty.
projects.about: An AI judge evaluates your pet's name on creativity, phonetics, and 'would I yell this at a park' factor. Surprisingly addictive.
projects.why_i_made_this: My cat is named Breadstick and I needed external validation.

projects.name: Starstory Chronicles
projects.one_line_pitch: A collaborative worldbuilding engine where AI and humans write lore together.
projects.about: A living worldbuilding tool where you create civilizations, histories, and characters ??and AI fills in the gaps. Every world is shareable and forkable.
projects.why_i_made_this: I've been building fictional worlds in notebooks since I was 12. I wanted to see what happens when AI helps.

projects.name: WiFi Name Generator
projects.one_line_pitch: Generate passive-aggressive, hilarious, or cryptic WiFi network names.
projects.about: Pick a vibe (threatening, wholesome, nerdy, unhinged) and get WiFi name suggestions. Copy to clipboard. Annoy your neighbors.
projects.why_i_made_this: My neighbor's WiFi is called 'FBI Surveillance Van 2' and I needed to escalate.

projects.name: One Pixel Art
projects.one_line_pitch: A collaborative canvas where each visitor can only place one pixel per day.
projects.about: A 256x256 canvas that evolves one pixel at a time. No accounts, just your daily pixel. Watch patterns emerge over weeks.
projects.why_i_made_this: I was thinking about r/place but slower. What if collective art took patience?

projects.name: Meeting Excuse Bot
projects.one_line_pitch: Generate believable excuses to skip meetings. Calibrated by urgency level.
projects.about: Slide a scale from 'mildly inconvenient' to 'absolutely cannot attend' and get a perfectly worded Slack message. Includes follow-up responses.
projects.why_i_made_this: I had 7 meetings in one day. Something had to give.

projects.name: Ambient Worlds
projects.one_line_pitch: Procedurally generated ambient soundscapes from imaginary places.
projects.about: Type a place that doesn't exist ??'a library on the moon', 'a train station in a cloud' ??and hear what it sounds like. Generated with AI audio.
projects.why_i_made_this: I wanted background noise that didn't exist yet.

projects.name: README Roaster
projects.one_line_pitch: Paste your GitHub README and get a brutally honest code review of the vibes.
projects.about: Not a code review ??a vibe review. It judges your project's name, description, emoji usage, badge addiction, and whether your README has personality.
projects.why_i_made_this: Too many READMEs are just auto-generated templates. I wanted to celebrate the weird ones.

projects.name: Compliment Machine
projects.one_line_pitch: Press a button. Get a genuine, specific, weirdly touching compliment.
projects.about: One button. One compliment. But they're not generic ??they're oddly specific and feel like they were written by a friend who really sees you.
projects.why_i_made_this: I was having a bad day and wrote the compliment I wanted to hear. Then I made 500 more.

projects.name: Deadline Chicken
projects.one_line_pitch: A countdown timer that gets increasingly aggressive as your deadline approaches.
projects.about: Set a deadline. The timer starts calm. As time runs out, the page gets redder, the font gets bigger, and the messages get more unhinged.
projects.why_i_made_this: Normal timers don't stress me out enough. I needed emotional consequences.

projects.name: Tiny Museum
projects.one_line_pitch: A museum of objects that only exist on the internet.
projects.about: A curated collection of digital artifacts ??screenshots of deleted tweets, AI-generated objects, website fossils, and things that only ever existed as pixels.
projects.why_i_made_this: The internet is full of beautiful ephemeral things. I wanted to put some in a glass case.

projects.name: Vibe Check API
projects.one_line_pitch: An API that rates the vibe of any text on a scale from 'corporate email' to 'unhinged tweet'.
projects.about: POST any text and get back a vibe score, vibe category, and suggested improvements. Works great for checking if your email sounds too corporate.
projects.why_i_made_this: I kept sending emails that sounded like a press release. I needed an intervention.
